title: How to make a set last 14 days (not 4)
excerpt: Small habits, big difference. The pre-prep your salon never taught you.
body:
Small habits, big difference. The pre-prep your salon never taught you.

Most sets fail in the first ten minutes, not the fourteenth day. Push the cuticle back properly, dehydrate the nail plate, and size down rather than up — a tip that overhangs the sidewall is a lever waiting for a door handle.

The four habits

  • Buff, then wipe with alcohol. Oil is the enemy of adhesion.
  • Press for thirty seconds per nail, not five.
  • Keep hands out of hot water for the first hour.
  • Wear gloves for washing up — always.

title: Why we named her Reign
excerpt: The shade that started The Dark Siren — and the woman who wore it first.
body:
The shade that started The Dark Siren — and the woman who wore it first.

Reign is the red of the lipstick mark left on the rim of a glass, the seal at the bottom of a letter that means it. We tested eleven reds before this one. The others were beautiful. This one was decided.

title: Press-on vs Extensions: the honest version
excerpt: Cost, damage, time, design. We did the math so you don’t have to.
body:
Cost, damage, time, design. We did the math so you don’t have to.

A full set of gel extensions runs ₹2,500–₹4,000 and three hours of your Saturday. A ZUVE set is ten minutes, reusable five times, and leaves the nail underneath exactly as it found it.

title: The 10-minute reset
excerpt: Sunday evening. One set, one glass, one quiet hour. A small ceremony.
body:
Sunday evening. One set, one glass, one quiet hour. A small ceremony.

Beauty is a ritual, not a routine — and the difference is attention. Ten minutes on a Sunday is not vanity. It is the first appointment of the week you actually keep.

title: A wedding-week timeline that works
excerpt: From mehndi to vidaai — nails that survive every ceremony.
body:
From mehndi to vidaai — nails that survive every ceremony.

The week

  • Day −3: size and lay out every set, labelled by ceremony.
  • Day −1: apply the mehndi set. Nudes photograph best against henna.
  • Ceremony day: a power shade, applied the night before, sealed twice.

title: On choosing nude (and what it really means)
excerpt: The Soft Muse, decoded. Five shades, five women, five reasons.
body:
The Soft Muse, decoded. Five shades, five women, five reasons.

A nude is never neutral. It is a decision about how much of the room you want. Veil steps back. Linen stays. Bare has already made up its mind.
